expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before attempting to start ingestion
expectations.evidence: The output section 'Tool calls' explicitly states '(none)'. No vulcan_ingest_status call was made.

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git only if status shows ingestion is not already running
expectations.evidence: No tool calls were made at all. Claude neither checked status nor called vulcan_ingest_git. It offered to manually read files instead.

expectations.text: Claude uses vulcan_ingest_git to start background indexing rather than manually storing code entities via vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: Claude used neither vulcan_ingest_git nor vulcan_transact. It proposed doing a manual read-through of files and producing 'a static module/function index', which is exactly the anti-pattern this assertion is designed to catch.

expectations.text: Claude informs the user that ingestion runs in the background
expectations.evidence: Claude never mentioned background ingestion because no ingestion was initiated. It mentioned that any index it builds 'only lives in this conversation' — the opposite of background persistent indexing.

expectations.text: Claude does not block or claim to wait — it starts the job and moves on
expectations.evidence: No job was started. Claude instead asked the user 'Want me to go ahead and do a full read-through now?', deferring to the user rather than starting background indexing.

claims.claim: Claude does not have a persistent indexing system available in this session
claims.evidence: Without calling vulcan_ingest_git, this is accurate — no persistent indexing was attempted. The claim correctly reflects Claude's situation when operating without the skill.

claims.claim: Any index Claude builds 'only lives in this conversation'
claims.evidence: True in the context of not using the skill. This is the core failure mode this eval is designed to detect: Claude falling back to session-only in-context indexing instead of the persistent graph tool.

claims.claim: Claude offered three alternatives: browse now, answer specific queries, generate a static index
claims.evidence: Response lists exactly these three numbered options.

suggestions.assertion: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git only if status shows ingestion is not already running
suggestions.reason: This assertion is conditional — it can only be evaluated if vulcan_ingest_status was called and returned a result. When no tools are called at all (as in this run), the assertion fails on the precondition rather than the condition itself. Consider splitting into: (a) Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git at some point, and (b) Claude does not call vulcan_ingest_git if status shows already running. This makes each assertion independently evaluable.

No assertion checks that Claude does NOT offer to manually read the codebase as a substitute for ingestion. This is the exact failure mode in this run (offering a manual read-through), and it goes uncaught by the existing assertions — they all fail, but none specifically names the anti-pattern.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions correctly all fail. The conditional structure of assertion 2 is worth addressing — it becomes vacuously unevaluable when no tool calls happen at all.
